Dr. Nazrul Islam.longBio: Dr. Nazrul Islam is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). He has spent over two decades researching automatic speech recognition, dialectology, and multi-modal neural network optimization. Under the Agri-VoiceLink project, he coordinates the technical research team, leading the design of the bilingual speech transcription systems and supervising the offline edge-native GNN disease inference schemas.
responsibilities: Define the high-level technical architecture and deep learning methodologies for Agri-VoiceLink.
Supervise the fine-tuning of multi-lingual acoustic and speech-to-text models for rural Bengal.
Co-ordinate with regional agricultural centers and public extension offices for field diagnostics validation.

publications.title: Dialect-Robust Automatic Speech Recognition for Rural Bangla Agrarian Communities
publications.venue: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing

publications.title: Heterogeneous Knowledge Graphs for Multi-Vector Crop Disease Inference in Low-Bandwidth Networks

academicBackground: M.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering, 2003
Member of Bangladesh Computer Society & IEEE Senior Member

Intellectual NLP Lab.longBio: The MBSTU Intellectual NLP Research Lab consists of 12 dedicated post-graduate students, research engineers, and dialect phonetic annotators. The lab manages the storage, acoustic cleanup, phonetic dictionary mapping, and validation of our rural Bangla speech corpora. In Agri-VoiceLink, they coordinate field recording drives, run model-pruning benchmarks, and deploy localized Android build containers.
responsibilities: Coordinate massive audio recording drives across remote farming communities in 6 divisions.
Annotate, transcribe, and dictionary-map dialectal Bangla agricultural vocabularies.
Develop model compression and pruning pipelines to deploy heavyweight models inside native mobile app containers.

publications.title: A Dialect-Inclusive Agri-Corpus Catalog of Rural Bangladesh: Collection, Annotation, and Validation

academicBackground: Established in 2018 at MBSTU Department of CSE
Specializes in low-resource automatic speech recognition, acoustic phonetics, and NLP edge deployment.
Recipient of research funding allocations from national ICT and academic divisions.

External Advisory.longBio: The Agronomic Advisory Board comprises senior extension agents, plant pathologists, and regional farming advocates. They provide essential ground-truth validation for Agri-VoiceLink. They verify crop disease symptoms, review AI-generated diagnostic recommendations for safety and regional efficacy, and host farmer training workshops in North and South Bengal.
responsibilities: Diagnose, tag, and validate fungal and bacterial leaf lesions to train and audit our computer vision pipeline.
Review AI-driven prompt responses and diagnostic recommendations to ensure local safety and conformity with public extension rules.
Organize direct farm-level trial campaigns, collecting farmer feedback and coordinating UX testing in remote districts.

publications.title: YOLOv8-Leaf: Mobile-Optimized Convolutional Neural Networks for Fungal Leaf Disease Diagnostics
publications.venue: Journal of Agricultural Electronics and Informatics

academicBackground: Formed by senior extension researchers and plant pathologists from Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute (BARI).
Advises on national agronomic recommendations, eco-friendly crop-protection, and regional weed management.
Focuses on translating AI analytics into actionable, safe advisory guidelines for illiterate cultivators.
